description: Create a two-column content slide on accessibility of automation with explanatory text on the left and a vertical bar chart on executive plans to make Gen AI accessible for automation on the right.

header.kicker: Technology Vision 2025 | AI: A Declaration of Autonomy
header.headline: Accessibility of Automation

items.sub: Generative AI is making automation more accessible. With LLMs and other foundation models, employees are accessing the insights and expertise of AI with unprecedented ease. In coming years, the advent of agents will exponentially grow this capability, as AI moves from insight to action and more enterprise systems become imbued with autonomy. And with open-source models also gaining momentum, soon more people and enterprises everywhere will be able to access cutting edge intelligence.

items.text: How does it connect to the trend?
items.sub: AI’s growing accessibility is what’s driving bottom-up autonomy in the workforce. While we’ve had no-code/low-code in the past, the adoption and utilization of today’s natural language-driven AI tools is growing far more rapidly and will touch far more types of workforce tasks. People are already bringing it to work—the question for enterprises is how to leverage these capabilities and workers’ enthusiasm to reimagine their strategies.

Camunda, a workflow automation provider, is adding AI capabilities to its platform. One feature called Copilot Suggestions lets employees describe a process in natural language, and then uses AI to automatically translate it into Business Process Modeling Notation, the industry-standard graphical language. On the open source side, Semantic Kernel is an open source software development kit created by Microsoft, letting developers bridge the gap between LLMs and existing software applications. Via plugins, LLMs can understand a request and write a call to execute an action within a piece of software, letting agents directly operate the application.

right.title: Executives plan to make Gen AI accessible for automation

footer.source: Accenture Technology Vision 2025 Executive Survey, N=4,021
